title: CI: Failed to upload artifacts after multiple tries: rpc error: code = Internal desc =
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Seeing a lot of CI flakes recently with this error:
```
Uploading 1 artifacts for /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/*.html
Uploaded /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/compose-podman-fedora-37-rootless-host.log.html
Error from upload stream: %!s(<nil>)
Failed to upload artifacts: rpc error: code = Internal desc = 
Re-trying to upload artifacts...Uploading 1 artifacts for /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/*.html
Uploaded /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/compose-podman-fedora-37-rootless-host.log.html
Error from upload stream: %!s(<nil>)
Failed to upload artifacts: rpc error: code = Internal desc = 
Re-trying to upload artifacts...
Failed to upload artifacts after multiple tries: rpc error: code = Internal desc = 
```

Rerunning the job works so it is a flake.

Some logs from today:
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5751613239853056
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5213705630121984

but I saw it a lot more often this week

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

no idea, only happens sometimes

comments.body: Same here.  Have seen it a lot in the past days.

Well damn, it's different artifacts it has trouble with too.  `html` here and `repo.tbz` [there](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16862#issuecomment-1354450850).  CI heavily depends on the `repo.tbz` artifact so simply disabling it as a workaround will break things.  I'll contact Cirrus-CI support and CC you guys, I don't think there's anything we can do from our end.
Update: This issue should be fixed on the Cirrus-CI end, sometimes next week.  I'll check back in when I return from PTO in 3 weeks.

I saw this again for the first time after the holidays:
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6721358504132608
https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5118141869588480

So the way this use to work (in theory, AIUI) the cirrus-agent process running on the host talks to the cirrus mothership.  The mothership in turn (because of our repo. config and .cirrus.yml details) talks to our own google-cloud storage bucket to store the artifacts.  
So there are multiple failure modes and it's difficult to diagnose after-the-fact where any given breakdown is happening.

Before the holidays I contacted Cirrus-support.  They [indicated a change](https://github.com/cirruslabs/cirrus-ci-agent/pull/263) to a more direct (agent -> storage) setup was forthcoming mid-December, though I have no details on when/if/where/how it actually happened in their production cluster.  Looking at the commit-history on that repo, I see there was a somewhat related change two week ago.

I can check our GCS logs to see if any errors were reported during those last few incidents.  Otherwise I s'pose the only thing to do is report the new occurrences to Cirrus-support.

According to Cirrus, roll-out of the new upload scheme was delayed until the 13th.  So this seems very likely to be the reason for additional occurrences.  Nevertheless, it's deployed now, but I'll leave this issue open just in case it happens again.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I have not seen it since so closing
